title: cerastium repens subsp. lineare
intro: Cerastium repens subsp. lineare, also known as the creeping or lineal mouse-ear chickweed, is a perennial plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae that grows low to the ground with linear leaves and white, star-shaped flowers. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, and has been employed to treat various health issues such as wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Cerastium repens subsp. lineare are attributed to its ability to soothe and heal skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote tissue regeneration, making it a popular choice for topical applications. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be done under the guidance of a medical professional due to potential interactions with other treatments.
